title: typeof expressions should only be compared to valid values
description: "typeof" expressions should only be compared to valid valuesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?More InfoThetypeofoperator returns a string indicating the type of its argument, and the set of returned values is limited:"undefined""boolean""number""string""symbol"(since ECMAScript 2015)"function""object"(fornulland any other object)"bigint"(since ECMAScript 2020)Compare atypeofexpression to anything else, and the result will always befalse.function isNumber(x) { return typeof x === "Number"; // Noncompliant: the function always returns 'false' }Instead, make sure you are always comparing the expression against one of the seven possible values.function isNumber(x) { return typeof x === "number"; }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect iss
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: typeof should only be compared to valid values: 'undefined', 'boolean', 'number', 'string', 'symbol', 'function', 'object', 'bigint'
typeof should only be compared to valid values: 'undefined', 'boolean', 'number', 'string', 'symbol', 'function', 'object', 'bigint' (reversed comparison)
